niit._note: NIIT thresholds are NOT indexed for inflation — fixed in statute since 2013

rates_individual._TODO_amt: Rev. Proc. 2025-XX. Exemption amounts and phaseout thresholds index annually. OBBBA made TCJA AMT parameters permanent.
rates_individual._note_qbi_rate_2026: OBBBA raises §199A QBI deduction rate from 20% to 23% effective tax year 2026

section_174_rd._note_obbba: OBBBA permanently restored §174 domestic R&D expensing. Foreign R&D remains 15-year capitalized. AUDIT FLASH POINT — confirm no further legislative changes.

rates_corporate._note_beat_rate_2026: OBBBA scheduled BEAT rate increase from 10% to 12.5% effective 2026

rates_corporate._note_fdii_2026: OBBBA scheduled §250 FDII deduction reduction from 37.5% to 21.875% effective 2026 (effective FDII rate rises from 13.125% to 16.406%)

_OBBBA_KNOWN_2026_CHANGES_SUMMARY: QBI deduction rate rises from 20% to 23% (§199A)
§250 GILTI deduction drops from 50% to 37.5% (effective rate rises to 13.125%)
§250 FDII deduction drops from 37.5% to 21.875% (effective rate rises to 16.406%)
§174 domestic R&D remains expensed (OBBBA permanent); foreign R&D still capitalized 15 years
Estate/gift exemption — TCJA sunset CANCELLED by OBBBA; remains at doubled level (indexed)
SALT cap indexes by 1% to ~$40,400; phaseout above $500k MAGI continues
§6041 information return threshold rises from $600 to $2,000
ARPA-enhanced PTC subsidies expire — reverts to pre-ARPA cliff unless extended
SECURE 2.0 Roth-only catch-up for high earners (>$145k) becomes mandatory
